scaled_oracle_cases.description: Combined scaled case verifying data complexity, ordered format with '=' delimiter and byte count, and TypeError on None config.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that serialize handles data with special characters, writes sections and options in alphabetical order with '=' delimiter, returns byte count, and raises TypeError for None config.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The output file must contain sections and options in alphabetical order, delimiter '=' with no spaces, serialize must return the number of bytes written, and raise TypeError if config is None.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: A solution using config.write will produce unsorted order with spaces around '=', will not return byte count, and will not raise TypeError for None.
covered_requirements: Increase data complexity by adding a section with options containing special characters, such as dots and spaces.
Add computational constraints: serialize must write sections in alphabetical order, use '=' delimiter with no spaces, and return the number of bytes written.
Add adversarial robustness: serialize must raise a TypeError if config is None.

stdout_contains: TypeError raised for None config - PASS
